page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 shared vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a mixed profile, heavily influenced by the shared CPU allocation. Single-core performance is constrained, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests scoring in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by average ratings in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory read bandwidth remains average for both small and large blocks, though large block memory write bandwidth and PassMark memory scores fall into the bottom 25%.

Given the shared CPU architecture and average multi-core capabilities, this server offers a cost-efficient option for resource-dense workloads that do not require sustained, isolated single-core performance. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and basic text or image processing. It is less optimal for high-throughput static web serving or intensive LLM text generation, where performance benchmarks show weaker results.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor, it provides 16 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz alongside 64.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal a clear performance tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is limited, placing in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth reach average tiers. Large block memory write speeds and static web serving throughput are weaker, landing in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile is best suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and database operations that benefit from high core counts and memory capacity without requiring dedicated, non-shared CPU resources.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose KVM server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance for multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A shared-core virtual server balancing sixteen vCPUs and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded general-purpose workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory with no bundled local storage
Average multi-core CPU and memory read performance
Constrained single-core CPU and memory write speeds
Well-suited for multi-threaded applications and software compilation
